summary: All features verified successfully. Backend: 14/14 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All pages load correctly with expected functionality. Key fixes confirmed: 1) Drill completion count uses Set for unique drill_id count (line 148 DrillsPage.jsx), 2) Pinnacle users get unlimited coaching (is_unlimited_coaching=true, coaching_sessions_remaining=-1), 3) Basic Plan and Last Mile have 4 peer sessions per month, 4) Startup migrations run on every backend restart, 5) Favicon properly configured.
critical_code_review_comments: DrillsPage.jsx line 148: Correctly uses Set to count unique drill_ids - `new Set(drillHistory.map(h => h.drill_id)).size`
startup_migrations.py: Runs on every backend startup via server.py lifespan context manager
server.py lines 55-56: Imports and runs startup migrations in lifespan startup
Pinnacle plan in database has coaching_sessions=-1 and peer_sessions_per_month=-1 (unlimited)
Basic Plan and Last Mile correctly configured with peer_sessions_per_month=4

Pro+ User.note: Login via API works, Google OAuth required for UI login

seed_data_creation: Used existing mock-login endpoints for testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: All bug fixes verified. Startup migrations run on every backend restart and update plan configurations. Pinnacle users get unlimited coaching. Basic Plan and Last Mile have 4 peer sessions. Drill completion count uses Set for unique counting.

drill_completion_count_fix.details: DrillsPage.jsx line 148 uses Set to count unique drill_ids instead of total attempts

pinnacle_unlimited_coaching.details: Pinnacle user dashboard shows 'Unlimited sessions' and 'Unlimited coaching'

basic_plan_peer_access.details: Basic Plan has peer_sessions_per_month=4 in database and API

last_mile_peer_access.details: Last Mile has peer_sessions_per_month=4 in database and API

startup_migrations.details: Backend logs show 'Running startup migrations...' and 'Plan configurations: 11 plans updated/created' on every restart

favicon_configuration.details: favicon.ico exists in /app/frontend/public/ and is accessible via HTTP (200 OK)

mocked_apis.note: All APIs use real MongoDB data. Mock-login endpoints are for testing purposes only.
